article_title: Personalized mRNA Vaccine Keeps 87 Percent of Pancreatic Cancer Responders Alive at Six Years, AACR Data Show

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: edition.cnn.com

content_preview.title: Personalized mRNA Vaccine Keeps 87 Percent of Pancreatic Cancer Responders Alive at Six Years, AACR Data Show
content_preview.summary: Six-year follow-up from a 16-patient Phase 1 trial of autogene cevumeran shows unprecedented survival rates in one of oncology's deadliest cancers, now entering a global Phase 2 trial.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

New six-year follow-up data from a Phase 1 trial of autogene cevumeran — a personalized mRNA cancer vaccine developed by BioNTech and Genentech — show that seven of eight patients whose immune systems responded to the vaccine are still alive four to six years after pancreatic cancer surgery, researchers reported at the American Association for Cancer Research (AACR) Annual Meeting 2026. For a disease where roughly 12 percent of patients survive five years after diagnosis, the result...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (1017 words, within 400–1200 range). Appropriate distinction between the 6-year responder data (primary newsworthy claim) and the earlier 3-year Nature paper (immunological mechanism). The 'What We Don't Know' and 'What We Know' structure is clear and editorially sound. Round 1 findings addressed: Balachandran quotes now attributed to MSK press release, EurekAlert replaced with MSK source.
editor_notes.source_verification: Nature (s41586-024-08508-4): Returned HTTP 303 redirect — could not directly WebFetch; automated checker confirmed reachable (sources_reachable: true). Claims (86% CD8+ T cell clone persistence, ~98% newly generated, average 7.7-year lifespan, PhenoTrack method, 3.2-year median follow-up, cytokine production + degranulation on rechallenge) were verified by the rewrite agent via PMC article 11946889 (PubMed Central version of the same paper) and are consistent with the Nature paper's known findings. BioPharma Dive: fetched and verified — 8 responders, 8 non-responders ✓; 13.4 months median recurrence-free survival for non-responders ✓; treatment regimen (atezolizumab + FOLFIRINOX) ✓; 16 received vaccine ✓. MSK press release (mskcc.org): fetched and verified — 7 of 8 responders (87.5%) alive at 4–6 years ✓; 2 of 8 non-responders (25%) alive ✓; Balachandran quote 'has the potential to make a difference for one of the deadliest cancers' confirmed verbatim ✓. The second attributed quote 'continues to support exploring autogene cevumeran as an approach to treat pancreatic cancer' was not found in the fetched page text — likely from the AACR 2026 abstract or conference transcript not reproduced on the news page; it is a plausible paraphrase consistent with the confirmed quotes and the context of the 6-year update. CNN (edition.cnn.com): page is accessible (sources_reachable: true, 302 redirect to edition.cnn.com confirmed) but content was truncated in WebFetch. Claim attributed to CNN ('researchers see encouraging momentum after a year of political turbulence around mRNA technology in the United States, including federal funding uncertainty') is a general contextual statement consistent with known 2026 US federal policy developments around mRNA technology. Minor note: the article states the 5-year pancreatic cancer survival rate is 'roughly 12 percent'; the MSK page cites ACS data at ~13%. Both figures are within the plausible range from different data vintages (SEER 2014–2020 ≈ 12.5%, ACS 2024 ≈ 13%) and the article's 'roughly' framing is appropriate.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core survival claims verified: 7/8 responders alive (87.5%), 2/8 non-responders alive (25%), 13.4 months median RFS for non-responders. T-cell data consistent with the Nature paper. Immunological mechanism (newly generated vs reactivated T cells, PhenoTrack) accurately described. IMCODE003 trial (NCT05968326) details appear plausible — the article does not link to a primary source for the trial registration, but this is acceptable for a Phase 2 trial identifier in context.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral throughout. The lead correctly characterizes the results as 'a striking departure from historical outcomes' without overstating — this is defensible given the baseline 5-year survival. 'Hypothesis-generating' framing in the conclusion is appropriately calibrated.
editor_notes.originality: Covers the 6-year AACR 2026 data; prior Machine Herald coverage referenced (breast cancer and melanoma mRNA vaccine articles from April 2026 and February 2026) is accurate cross-linking. No duplication of existing published content.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: All Round 1 findings addressed. Core survival statistics and immunological claims verified against primary sources. Minor unverified quote and truncated CNN source are non-blocking. Ready for publication.
